title: Life expectancy vs. expected years lived with disability or disease
caption:
Life expectancy vs. expected years lived with disability or disease

The number of years a person is expected to live from birth, assuming they will experience the age-specific mortality rate observed in a given year throughout their lifetime.

This scatter plot displays 2023 data comparing average life expectancy against expected years lived with disability or disease for various countries. The vertical axis measures life expectancy ranging from 40 to over 80 years, while the horizontal axis tracks expected years lived with disability or disease from 7 to 13 years. Circles are sized by population, with a reference scale indicating sizes for 1.4 billion and 600 million people. A color-coded legend identifies regions: North America in orange, South America in brown, Africa in purple, Europe in blue, Asia in teal, and Oceania in light blue. In the upper right quadrant, Japan, France, and the United States show life expectancies above 80 years paired with 12 to 13 years of expected disability or disease. China appears as the largest circle, positioned near 78 years of life expectancy and roughly 10.5 years of disability. India and Pakistan sit in the middle of the chart with life expectancies around 70 to 72 years and disability durations near 10.5 years. African nations such as Nigeria, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of Congo cluster in the lower left, with life expectancies between 55 and 65 years and disability periods around 9 to 10 years. Laos and Nauru appear on the far left with the lowest recorded years of disability, near 7 to 7.5 years. The distribution shows a general trend where higher life expectancy correlates with a higher number of years lived with disability or disease.
